title: US and Iran Seek Path to Peace Amid New Tensions
subtitle: Negotiators work to keep diplomacy alive as leaders trade threats and sanctions are temporarily lifted.

summary.en: High-stakes talks between the United States and Iran continue in Switzerland despite significant challenges. President Donald Trump’s aggressive social media posts caused a temporary walkout by the Iranian delegation. Meanwhile, the US has issued a temporary waiver to ease oil sanctions on Iran. Both sides have agreed to a 60-day roadmap to reach a final deal. However, disagreements remain regarding nuclear inspections and regional military actions.
summary.zh: 儘管面臨重大挑戰，美國與伊朗在瑞士的高風險談判仍在持續。川普總統激進的社群媒體貼文曾導致伊朗代表團短暫離席。與此同時，美國已發布臨時豁免令以放寬對伊朗的石油制裁。雙方已同意制定一份六十天的藍圖以達成最終協議。然而，關於核子檢查與區域軍事行動的分歧依然存在。

paragraphs.en: Negotiations between the United States and Iran have reached a critical stage in Switzerland, as both nations attempt to resolve deep-seated conflicts. While the talks are moving forward, the process has been marked by a series of tense moments and public disagreements between the two sides.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗在瑞士的談判已進入關鍵階段，雙方正試圖解決根深蒂固的衝突。儘管會談持續推進，但過程中充滿了緊張時刻，且雙方不時公開產生分歧。

paragraphs.en: The current round of discussions began with a major disruption. According to The Guardian, the Iranian negotiating team walked out of the talks to protest a series of threats made by US President Donald Trump on social media. The president had threatened to bomb Iran and even suggested he might kidnap the Iranian team if the Strait of Hormuz—a vital waterway for global oil shipments—was not reopened. Despite this, mediators from Qatar and Pakistan worked behind the scenes to keep the dialogue alive, and high-level meetings eventually continued.
paragraphs.zh: 本輪討論以一場重大干擾揭開序幕。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，伊朗談判團隊為抗議美國總統川普（Donald Trump）在社群媒體上發出的一系列威脅而憤而離席。川普曾威脅要轟炸伊朗，甚至暗示若荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）——這條全球石油運輸的生命線——未重新開放，他可能會綁架伊朗談判代表。儘管如此，來自卡達與巴基斯坦的調停者仍在幕後努力維持對話，高層會議最終得以繼續進行。

paragraphs.en: Despite the heated rhetoric, there has been some progress. BBC News reports that the US and Iran have agreed to a roadmap aimed at reaching a final agreement within 60 days. As part of this effort, the US Treasury has issued a 60-day waiver that eases long-standing oil sanctions. This move allows Iran to sell oil in US dollars and trade more freely on the international market for the first time in many years. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent noted that this relief is tied to Iran’s commitment to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and to allow international nuclear inspectors back into the country.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管言詞激烈，談判仍取得了一些進展。BBC新聞報導指出，美伊雙方已同意制定一份藍圖，目標是在六十天內達成最終協議。作為努力的一部分，美國財政部已發布一項為期六十天的豁免令，放寬了長期以來的石油制裁。此舉使伊朗多年來首次能以美元銷售石油，並在國際市場上更自由地進行貿易。美國財政部長貝森特（Scott Bessent）表示，這項緩解措施與伊朗承諾保持荷莫茲海峽暢通，並允許國際核子檢查人員重返該國掛鉤。

paragraphs.en: However, the path to a final deal remains complicated by conflicting statements. Vice-President JD Vance, who has been involved in the negotiations, suggested that discussions regarding the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) inspectors could begin immediately. Yet, Iran’s foreign ministry quickly denied this, stating that Tehran had made no new commitments regarding nuclear inspections. A spokesperson for the Iranian foreign ministry told state media that any future engagement with UN inspectors would only happen under existing rules set by their own government, not as part of a new deal.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，通往最終協議的道路仍因相互矛盾的聲明而變得複雜。參與談判的副總統范斯（JD Vance）暗示，關於國際原子能總署（IAEA）檢查人員的討論可以立即展開。但伊朗外交部迅速否認，聲稱德黑蘭當局並未就核子檢查做出任何新承諾。伊朗外交部發言人向官媒表示，未來與聯合國檢查人員的任何接觸，都將僅在該國政府既有的規則下進行，而非作為新協議的一部分。

paragraphs.en: Regional conflicts also continue to threaten the success of these talks. Iran has expressed deep frustration over ongoing Israeli military actions in Lebanon. According to The Guardian, Iran claims that these attacks violate a memorandum of understanding signed last week, which was intended to support a ceasefire—an agreement to stop fighting—on all fronts. While the US and Iran have agreed to create a communication line to avoid accidents in the Strait of Hormuz and a special cell to help manage the situation in Lebanon, the reality on the ground remains volatile.
paragraphs.zh: 區域衝突也持續威脅著這些會談的成功。伊朗對以色列在黎巴嫩持續進行的軍事行動表達了深切的不滿。根據《衛報》報導，伊朗聲稱這些攻擊違反了上週簽署的諒解備忘錄，該備忘錄旨在支持各戰線的停火——即停止戰鬥的協議。雖然美伊雙方已同意建立一條溝通管道以避免在荷莫茲海峽發生意外，並成立一個特別小組來協助管理黎巴嫩局勢，但當地的現實情況依然動盪不安。

paragraphs.en: Inside the negotiating room, the atmosphere is equally complex. While President Trump has used aggressive language, Vice-President Vance has adopted a different tone, telling reporters that he was asked by the president to use these talks to start a new chapter in relations with Iran. Some observers note that the Iranian delegation feels significant pressure at home to show they do not trust the American team, which explains their decision to walk out during the earlier protests.
paragraphs.zh: 在談判室內，氣氛同樣複雜。雖然川普總統採取了強硬的言詞，但范斯副總統則採取了不同的態度，他告訴記者，總統要求他利用這些會談開啟與伊朗關係的新篇章。一些觀察家指出，伊朗代表團在國內面臨巨大壓力，必須展現出他們不信任美方團隊的態度，這解釋了他們在稍早抗議期間選擇離席的決定。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the next few weeks will be a major test for both nations. The 60-day waiver on sanctions provides a window of opportunity, but it is clear that both sides are still struggling to build mutual trust. President Trump has warned that if Iran does not live up to its agreements or continues to cause trouble, he will take further action. Meanwhile, Iranian officials have dismissed the American threats, suggesting that such pressure has not worked in the past and will not work now.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，接下來的幾週對兩國而言都將是重大考驗。為期六十天的制裁豁免提供了一個機會之窗，但顯然雙方仍在努力建立互信。川普總統警告，若伊朗未能履行協議或持續製造麻煩，他將採取進一步行動。與此同時，伊朗官員駁斥了美國的威脅，暗示這種壓力過去無效，現在也不會奏效。

paragraphs.en: As the technical talks continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, the world is watching closely. The success of these negotiations depends on whether both countries can move past their public disagreements and focus on the practical steps needed to ensure regional stability. For now, the mediators from Qatar and Pakistan remain essential in keeping both sides at the table, hoping that the current roadmap will eventually lead to a lasting peace.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著技術性會談在瑞士持續進行至本週結束，全世界都在密切關注。這些談判的成功取決於兩國能否跨越公開分歧，並專注於確保區域穩定所需的實際步驟。目前，來自卡達與巴基斯坦的調停者在維持雙方對話方面仍扮演關鍵角色，期盼目前的藍圖最終能引領至持久和平。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To leave a meeting or event as a way of showing strong disapproval or protest.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （因抗議或不滿而）中途離場、退席。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的片語，但在新聞中常指抗議行為。文中：the Iranian negotiating team walked out of the talks to protest a series of threats made by US President Donald Trump on social media.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An official document that gives someone permission to ignore a rule or requirement for a specific period.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在法律或外交新聞中常見，指暫時免除某項規定。文中：As part of this effort, the US Treasury has issued a 60-day waiver that eases long-standing oil sanctions.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To do what is expected or promised.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語常與承諾或標準連用，表示沒有辜負期望。文中：President Trump has warned that if Iran does not live up to its agreements or continues to cause trouble, he will take further action.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A small group of people working together for a specific purpose, often within a larger organization.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「細胞」或「牢房」，但在外交或軍事語境下指特定任務小組。文中：While the US and Iran have agreed to create a communication line to avoid accidents in the Strait of Hormuz and a special cell to help manage the situation in Lebanon, the reality on the ground remains volatile.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: US eases oil sanctions as Iran denies Vance claim on nuclear inspectors

previousArticles.title: US-Iran Talks Face New Challenges as Tensions Rise in Switzerland
previousArticles.summaryEn: High-stakes negotiations between the United States and Iran have hit a difficult period in Switzerland. Iranian officials walked out of the talks to protest aggressive social media posts from President Donald Trump. The dispute centers on the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane that Iran claims to have closed. Meanwhile, fighting continues in southern Lebanon between Israeli forces and Hezbollah. Mediators from Qatar and Pakistan are working to keep the diplomatic process alive. Both sides remain under pressure to honor a recent agreement to end the war.

previousArticles.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Threatens to Close Vital Shipping Route
previousArticles.summaryEn: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to recent Israeli military strikes in Lebanon. This move threatens a fragile 60-day ceasefire agreement signed between the US and Iran just days ago. While Iran claims the waterway is closed, the US military insists that traffic is still moving normally. International leaders are now heading to Switzerland to continue peace talks despite the rising violence. The situation remains uncertain as both sides struggle to maintain the new peace deal.

quiz.question: What condition is attached to the 60-day sanctions waiver granted to Iran?

options.A: Iran must immediately cease all military actions in Lebanon.
options.B: Iran must ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open and allow nuclear inspectors access.
options.C: Iran must sign a new memorandum of understanding with the United States.
options.D: Iran must publicly apologize for the walkout during the initial negotiations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，美國財政部長 Scott Bessent 明確指出，這項減免制裁的措施是與伊朗承諾「保持荷姆茲海峽暢通」以及「允許國際核武檢查員重返該國」這兩項條件掛鉤的。選項 A 雖然提到黎巴嫩衝突，但並非制裁減免的條件；選項 C 的備忘錄與停火有關而非制裁條件；選項 D 文章中並未提及伊朗需要公開道歉作為條件。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the Iranian negotiating team be acting cautiously or aggressively during the talks?

options.A: They are confident that the US will remove all sanctions regardless of their actions.
options.B: They are following strict orders from the UN to avoid any direct contact with the US.
options.C: They are under domestic pressure to demonstrate a lack of trust toward the American delegation.
options.D: They want to ensure that the talks move to a different location other than Switzerland.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，文章指出伊朗代表團在國內承受巨大壓力，必須表現出對美國團隊的不信任，這解釋了他們為何在談判初期選擇離場抗議。選項 A 與事實相反，伊朗對美方仍持懷疑態度；選項 B 文章未提及聯合國對伊朗有此類指示；選項 D 關於談判地點的選擇並非伊朗採取強硬態度的原因。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'volatile' mean in the context of the situation in Lebanon?

options.B: Likely to change suddenly and unexpectedly.
options.D: Physically dangerous due to chemical weapons.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在第五段中，作者描述雖然美伊雙方建立了溝通管道，但當地局勢依然「volatile」。根據上下文，這指的是局勢不穩定且隨時可能惡化。選項 A 與原意相反；選項 C 雖然有調解人，但並未讓局勢變得可控；選項 D 雖然該地區危險，但 volatile 在此處是指局勢的「不穩定性」而非指化學物質的揮發性或危險性。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the US-Iran negotiations?

options.A: The negotiations have already successfully resulted in a lasting peace treaty.
options.B: The US and Iran have completely resolved their differences regarding nuclear inspections.
options.C: The talks are at a fragile, critical stage where progress is hindered by deep distrust and regional conflicts.
options.D: The mediators from Qatar and Pakistan have taken over the negotiations from the US and Iran.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章的主旨在於描述美伊談判雖然取得初步進展（如 60 天路線圖），但仍面臨巨大的不信任感、公開爭端以及區域衝突的挑戰，處於一個關鍵且脆弱的階段。選項 A 與事實不符，目前僅是路線圖而非最終條約；選項 B 錯誤，因為伊朗已否認對核檢查做出新承諾；選項 D 錯誤，調解人僅是協助對話，並未取代美伊雙方的談判地位。
